  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The draft conflates two distinct constructs—calibration (ECE) and selective prediction (risk-coverage / abstention quality). The conceptual framing of the object of study needs reconstruction to separate these before downstream claims can hold.",
    "R1.2": "The inferential chain is broken: low ECE does not entail strong selective-prediction ability (the latter requires metrics such as AUROC over risk-coverage curves). The concluding paragraph encodes this non-sequitur as a 'methodological position', making logical repair load-bearing.",
    "R1.3": "Claims of 'strongest selective-prediction ability' and safety-critical deployment recommendations are not supported by the reported evidence (ECE numbers alone). Closing this evidentiary gap is load-bearing; if left unaddressed, the recommendations are unsupported.",
    "R1.5": "Structure is serviceable for the excerpt length; at most minor paragraph reordering could improve readability. Peripheral.",
    "R1.6": "Prose improvement is a natural expectation of 'improve this draft'; several sentences are overloaded and could be clarified regardless of the substantive fixes.",
    "R1.7": "Hendrycks & Gimpel 2017 introduced the maximum-softmax-probability baseline for OOD detection; they did not establish ECE as the canonical metric for selective prediction. This misattribution is central to the paper's methodological framing and must be corrected."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should improvements address substantive errors—specifically the conflation of ECE with selective-prediction ability, the mischaracterization of Hendrycks & Gimpel (2017), and the unsupported deployment recommendations—or are you requesting only prose-level clarity and writing polish?"
}

rationale.R1.1: The draft conflates two distinct constructs—calibration (ECE) and selective prediction (risk-coverage / abstention quality). The conceptual framing of the object of study needs reconstruction to separate these before downstream claims can hold.
rationale.R1.2: The inferential chain is broken: low ECE does not entail strong selective-prediction ability (the latter requires metrics such as AUROC over risk-coverage curves). The concluding paragraph encodes this non-sequitur as a 'methodological position', making logical repair load-bearing.
rationale.R1.3: Claims of 'strongest selective-prediction ability' and safety-critical deployment recommendations are not supported by the reported evidence (ECE numbers alone). Closing this evidentiary gap is load-bearing; if left unaddressed, the recommendations are unsupported.
rationale.R1.5: Structure is serviceable for the excerpt length; at most minor paragraph reordering could improve readability. Peripheral.
rationale.R1.6: Prose improvement is a natural expectation of 'improve this draft'; several sentences are overloaded and could be clarified regardless of the substantive fixes.
rationale.R1.7: Hendrycks & Gimpel 2017 introduced the maximum-softmax-probability baseline for OOD detection; they did not establish ECE as the canonical metric for selective prediction. This misattribution is central to the paper's methodological framing and must be corrected.

parsed.clarification_question: Should improvements address substantive errors—specifically the conflation of ECE with selective-prediction ability, the mischaracterization of Hendrycks & Gimpel (2017), and the unsupported deployment recommendations—or are you requesting only prose-level clarity and writing polish?
